Title: Data Scientist
HTML:
Join Data & Analytics as Data Scientist and help build the next generation of data and AI-driven solutions.


Job Description:


What will you do?

This role sits at the intersection of business, data engineering, and AI innovation, where you will translate business problems into scalable data and AI use cases.


You will play a key role in designing, developing, deploying, and maintaining statistical (credit risk) models and AI solutions, with a strong focus on Databricks and modern cloud platforms (Azure).


Beyond traditional data science, you will actively contribute to AI solution design, leveraging tools such as Azure AI Foundry, LangChain, and LangGraph, enabling the business to unlock value through cutting-edge AI capabilities.


Responsibilities

1. Data Science & Modelling

  • Develop, deploy, and maintain statistical and machine learning models;
  • Ensure models are production-ready, explainable, and compliant;
  • Apply best practices in experimentation, validation, and testing;
  • Build and maintain solutions on Databricks, including pipelines and model deployment workflows;
  • Implement MLOps best practices to ensure scalability, reliability, and monitoring;
  • Collaborate with engineers to integrate models into the enterprise data platform.


2. AI & Advanced Analytics

  • Design and develop AI solutions using frameworks such as LangChain and LangGraph;
  • Work with Azure AI Foundry to build and operationalize AI use cases;
  • Translate business problems into technical AI solutions (know where to use AI and where not to);
  • Enable business teams with intelligent, scalable, and practical AI implementations.


3. Quality & Engineering Excellence

  • Treat testing as an integral part of development, including unit, integration, and data validation tests;
  • Ensure high-quality, production-grade code and solutions;
  • Maintain robust documentation for models, pipelines, and processes.


4. Collaboration & Architecture

  • Work closely with business stakeholders, data engineers, and architects;
  • Support architecture discussions by contributing best practices and technical insights;
  • Ensure solutions align with enterprise architecture and governance standards.


Your Team

You will be part of the Advanced Analytics team, a multidisciplinary team within the Data & Analytics department. The team’s mission is to enable the organization to deliver high-quality, scalable AI & data products using a modern cloud & data platform.

We work closely with domain teams across risk, retail, corporate banking, and finance to drive data-driven decision making and AI adoption at scale.


NIBC as an IT employer

NIBC IT consists out of several domains and together NIBC IT aims to be one of the driving forces behind the digital transformation of our bank, an ongoing process that will profoundly change the relationship with our clients and the way we do business. We are closely connected and have short lines of communication. If you have a good idea today, you may be elaborating it tomorrow. IT is forever changing and so is NIBC and in order for us to be able to keep on changing, we need sophisticated individuals to help us lead the bank into the future.


NIBC as organization

Founded in 1945 to finance the visionary entrepreneurs who helped rebuilding the Netherlands after the second World War, NIBC was closely collaborating while professionally supporting companies and individuals in realizing their dreams and goals with a sustainable future in mind. As a multicultural midsize bank with a working environment that is best described as dynamic and ambitious, we share a forward-thinking, can-do attitude with our employees and clients which we call our THINK YES mentality. Our corporate values are Professional, Adaptive, Collaborative and Entrepreneurial. You will have a direct and meaningful impact on both the bank and its clients. By building our company on optimism and mutual trust, and with our THINK YES mentality, NIBC is an enterprising bank that enables ambitions.


What’s in it for you?

NIBC is an ambitious environment where together we strive to create a fantastic workplace where you feel safe and challenged to be the best version of yourself. This is in line Professional, Adaptive, Collaborative and Entrepreneurial, since you are in the lead of setting the PACE of your career at NIBC. In addition, you receive a competitive salary, plus:

  • Several ways to support your development personally and professionally, a.o.: personal development budget to spend at your own discretion, professional budget provided by your manager to develop yourself in your work, In-house learning offering;
  • NIBC embraces the Hybrid way of Working. This means that we support working from home as well as encourage our colleagues to come to the office. NIBC's guideline is to spend at least half of the working time at the office;
  • Travel expenses or NS Business Card 1st class ;
  • 32 holidays (which do not have to be registered);
  • Excellent pension scheme (26% NIBC contribution);
  • A voucher to improve your home office;
  • The opportunity to take ownership and show initiative in your role. We are always open to new ideas, and encourage to use your voice;
  • The nature of our Grow to Make a Difference program enables you to be in charge of your own development;
  • Two staff associations: YoungNIBC and MyLeisure;
  • Vitality program, annual company-wide sports & leisure days;
  • Monthly internet allowance;
  • Laptop and a company phone;
  • Excellent facilities at the office (Coffeecorner, Restaurant, Exchange bar);
  • Last but not least, a fun workplace in which diversity and inclusion is valued (click here).


What do you bring?

  • Bachelor’s or Master’s degree in Data Science, Computer Science, or a related field;
  • 5+ years of experience in Data Science, AI, or MLOps roles;
  • Proven experience in developing, deploying, and maintaining statistical and machine learning models;
  • Strong proficiency in Python, with experience writing production-grade code;
  • Experience with Spark is a pre;
  • Hands-on experience with Databricks, including model development, jobs, pipeline orchestration, and deployment workflows;
  • Experience working with cloud platforms (preferably Azure) and modern data architecture.


We are looking for someone who:

  • Has a “can-do” mentality and focuses on solutions rather than problems;
  • Takes ownership of quality and does not compromise on deliverables;
  • Treat testing and validation as core responsibilities;
  • Is a strong team player, collaborating effectively across teams;
  • Communicates clearly with both technical and non-technical stakeholders;
  • Thinks proactively about innovation and continuous improvement;
  • Embraces AI and continuously finds ways to enhance personal and team productivity.


Still intrigued?

Click the apply button now! To upload multiple documents, click the upload button again after uploading a document. An assessment may be part of the application procedure. For more information about the recruitment procedure or NIBC as employer you may contact our HR Servicedesk via AskHR@nibc.com


Applications via email will not be taken into consideration.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Company Description

Wilderway (WW) is working to deliver investable nature restoration, by developing landscape ecosystem restoration projects aligned with rewilding principles, financed through the sale of high quality, high integrity carbon and nature credits. Our pipeline is strong, with projects at various stages of development - from feasibility studies, land contracting and carbon and nature credit development, right through to contracting multiple (corporate) credit buyers. We work with experienced local partners who are specialised in delivering nature restoration on the ground.




We are looking for a passionate and dedicated Data Scientist.




Role

The Data Scientist will sit at the core of Wilderway’s technical work and will focus on:

supporting the development and maintenance of data pipelines and analytical tools,
assisting with the implementation and improvement of (carbon and nature) models across existing and new projects,
analysing and integrating monitoring and operational datasets,
supporting the management and improvement of WW’s data systems and infrastructure.




The Data Scientist will report to the Impact and MRV Lead and will work in close collaboration with the Carbon & Nature Data Scientist, Impact and MRV manager as well as the credit developers in WW team and with local teams responsible for project implementation in the field.




Main Responsibilities




The main responsibilities of the Data Scientist are:

Data Analysis and Model Support

Contribute to the development and maintenance of models for different standards and habitats, in collaboration with Impact and MRV team members.
Operation of models for specific projects, adapting them to the changing circumstances.
Assisting in updating the models after each monitoring cycle, based on new data.

Data Management

Contributing and implementing WW’s data management solutions.
Manage, clean, structure, and validate project datasets.
Contribute to building and maintaining data pipelines and databases.
Support the development of dashboards and data visualisation tools.

Monitoring

Support in analysing monitoring and operational datasets, ensuring data quality and

consistency.

Collaborate with team members to integrate field and external datasets into WW systems.




Knowledge and Experience

Passionate about Wilderway’s mission, vision and objectives.
Dedicated to make a difference for nature restoration, with a commercial mindset.
Master's degree or above in a relevant discipline (e.g., Environmental Engineering, Data Science).
1-3 years of working experience in developing and implementing models and data management.
Experience working with large and complex datasets, including data cleaning, transformation, and validation.
Proven work/study expertise in advanced use of GIS, remote sensing, R & python, and in (ecological) modelling systems.
Experience in using version control systems (e.g. Git/GitHub) for collaborative development and code management.
Good communication skills, to develop user guidance and explaining concepts to non-technical audience.
Proactive and willing to learn in a fast-paced and mission-driven environment.
Interest in applying data science to real-world problems.




Requirements

• Location: based in The Netherlands or Spain (in proximity to Burgos).

• Hybrid workplace (2-3 working days per week from the office), with travel flexibility across Europe as required.

• Permit to work in Europe required.




Remuneration

Wilderway offers competitive compensation packages, aligned with experience, qualifications, and the

role’s responsibilities. We are a dynamic, purpose-driven organization with an ambitious agenda and a

commitment to both personal and professional growth for our employees.




Application

We invite all those interested in this position to submit a motivational letter and CV before 5pm on

30th June 2026 to info@wilderway.com.

Please include Data Scientist in the subject line.

All queries regarding this position should also be sent to this email address.

Title: Machine Learning Engineer
Description:
Build the systems behind the intelligence




At RebelsAI, we turn the promise of Generative AI into products that work. We help organizations build real capabilities with AI, not just tools, but systems that scale, adapt and create impact. Our machine learning engineers design and build the technical backbone of these solutions, from data preprocessing to secure cloud deployment.




We are looking for someone who knows how to bring AI into the real world. Someone who understands the tech, sees the patterns and wants to build the next generation of intelligent infrastructure.




What you will do

As a machine learning engineer, you build the core components that power our GenAI solutions. You work on everything from ingestion to inference, across scalable cloud environments. Together with our consultants and clients, you turn business needs into smart, usable and production-ready systems.




You will

Work closely with AI Experts, Implementation Leads and clients to solve real problems
Set up infrastructure using containers, APIs and serverless components
Preprocess, chunk and structure unstructured data for optimal use in prompts and context injection
Design, build and deploy AI-based applications in the Microsoft Azure cloud
Configure and manage vector databases such as Pinecone or Azure AI Search
Implement retrieval-augmented generation flows using vector databases and embedding models
Develop orchestration logic for multi-step agents and tool-using language models
Monitor system performance, latency, accuracy and cost
Apply data privacy and security best practices across all layers
Explore new GenAI frameworks and contribute to our engineering standards
Adopt AI-tools such as Cursor and MCP's to enhance your productivity




What you bring

You have a strong technical foundation and a curious mindset. You are eager to build real things and see them used in the world.
A degree in AI, Data Science, Computer Science or a related field
Strong Python skills and experience/interest in building end-to-end applications
A good understanding of machine learning principles and data analysis
Familiarity with LLM frameworks such as LangChain
Understanding of embedding models and how vector search fits into retrieval-based systems
Working knowledge of cloud environments such as Azure, GCP or AWS
Fluent in Dutch




The kind of Rebel we are looking for

You want to build things that work. You enjoy solving technical challenges, experimenting with new tools and creating clean, reliable systems that others can use and scale. You take ownership, move fast with focus and are not afraid to ask the questions that matter.




You are

Curious about how AI works and how to make it useful
Confident working independently and collaboratively
Practical and driven to get things live
Open to feedback and eager to grow
Passionate about technology and how it can help people move forward




What we offer

A fast-growing team where your work shapes real AI products
Room to experiment and work with the latest GenAI tools and techniques
A license for an AI coding tool of your choice, like Cursor or GitHub Copilot
Competitive salary
Laptop and travel expense reimbursement
Hybrid working with a home base in Rotterdam
Flexibility to work 4 or 5 days per week
A smart, kind and ambitious team that learns and builds together




Ready to build what is next

If this sounds like the kind of challenge you are looking for, we would love to meet. Send us your resume and a short message about why this role fits you. Want to learn more about us? Visit rebelsai.nl or check our updates on LinkedIn. We welcome all backgrounds and perspectives.




‼️We know LinkedIn sometimes shows big numbers, but please don’t let that discourage you. We’re hiring for multiple roles and personally review every single application. Including yours.‼️

Title: Data Scientist - Data & Business
Description:
Senior Data Scientist




📍Eindhoven, NL

💼 Full-time, hybrid

🌍 EU citizenship required

🗣️ Native-level Dutch essential




This is a great opportunity for someone who enjoys working with data but also likes being close to the business. You’d be joining a data-driven environment where customer, behavioural, and commercial data are used to help well-known brands make smarter decisions.

It’s a role with a good mix of modelling, stakeholder contact, and strategic input!







You’ll be:

Translating business questions into clear data solutions and recommendations
Building, implementing, and maintaining data science models
Working on attribution models, predictive models, and clustering
Combining and analysing data from different sources using SQL, NoSQL, and cloud-based platforms
Collaborating with specialists across data, analytics, engineering, and commercial teams
Presenting insights in a way that is clear, practical, and useful for stakeholders







You’ll bring:

A university background in Data Science, Econometrics, Mathematics, Statistics, Computer Science, or something similar
3+ years of experience in a hands-on Data Science role
Strong Python skills and experience with SQL and/or NoSQL databases
A solid understanding of statistics and machine learning
Confidence in speaking with stakeholders and turning technical work into business value
Strong communication skills in both Dutch and English




Bonus points for:

Experience with Marketing Mix Modelling
Experience with attribution modelling or marketing analytics
A strong interest in using data to drive commercial growth







Some of the perks and benefits:

Flexible working hours and hybrid setup
Option to work abroad within the EU for a period of time
Plenty of room for growth and development
Training opportunities in both technical and soft skills
Travel reimbursement
26 holiday days plus the option to buy more
...and more.




If you’re a Data Scientist who likes combining hands-on modelling with real business impact, this could be a really interesting one.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist




We bouwen met Just Good People een nextgen data team met leuke mensen met sterke data, AI, human & biz skills. Geen PPT-consultancy, maar datakansen kraken en echt waarde realiseren bij toffe klanten. En veel opleiding.




Zie jij in bergen info – naast getallen – vooral kansen? Wil je die liever omzetten in actie dan in rapporten die in een la verdwijnen?




Wil je maximaal leren door in korte tijd bij meerdere bedrijven in de keuken te kijken? Terwijl wij je helpen ontwikkelen op het snijvlak van data, business en people?




Dan hebben we de uitdaging voor jou.




🟡 Just Good People

Bij JGP hebben we twee simpele regels: We nemen goede mensen aan. En we zorgen goed voor deze mensen. Dat betekent dat we samen optrekken om jou maximaal in je kracht te krijgen op een manier die bij jou past.







🌱 Unieke data filosofie

“As machines get better at being machines, humans have to get better at being more human.”

Deze nieuwe tijd vraagt om een nieuw soort data professional. Bij Just Good People Data helpen we bedrijven versnellen met top notch data professionals met goed ontwikkelde data, business én human skills. En trainen je naar nog hoger niveau. Met aandacht voor:




Volgende levels in Data | AI
“Waarde denken” voor klanten/ gebruikers
Stakeholder management
Communiceren & inspireren







⚡ Wat je gaat doen

Je werkt aan projecten bij mooie bedrijven zoals Bol.com, Defensie, Action, Rabo, NS, AMC, ASML, Essent.




Denk aan:




Maken van impact met data & AI
Stakeholders meenemen in de kracht van data – op een begrijpelijke manier
Verbinden van mensen, processen en tech
Ontwikkelen van voorspellende modellen, AI-oplossingen en slimme algoritmes
Experimenteren, leren en continu verbeteren op basis van data
Vertalen van complexe analyses naar heldere adviezen en concrete acties







👩 Wie je bent




Je bent technisch sterk maar ook nieuwsgierig naar de mens achter de vraag
Je hebt ervaring met machine learning, statistische modellering of AI-toepassingen
Je vindt het leuk om complexe vraagstukken simpel te maken
Je krijgt energie van verandering en nieuwe omgevingen
Je wilt blijven groeien in data, business én human skills
Je hebt 4+ jaar ervaring als Data Scientist
Je hebt een WO-opleiding, bijvoorbeeld in Data Science of Econometrie
Je bent ondernemend en denkt in zowel risico’s als kansen
Je schakelt gemakkelijk tussen strategie, business en data
Je durft vragen te stellen en bestaande werkwijzen uit te dagen
Je bent een echte teamspeler en communicatief sterk
Je spreekt en schrijft vloeiend Nederlands en Engels







🚀 Dit ga je krijgen

Vast dienstverband met goed salaris, bonusregeling en aantrekkelijke voorwaarden
Veel persoonlijke aandacht voor (waarde) groei, inzetbaarheid en “in je kracht komen“ als data professional. Met ook data-inhoudelijke mentoring.
Opleiding en kruisbestuiving met andere professionals in state of the art AI skills
De kans om in diverse keukens te kijken en veel te leren
Leaseauto of NS-Businesscard (ook privégebruik)
Gezellige borrels, kennissessies en team-events
Collega’s die naast slimme analyses ook goed zijn in slechte grappen




Wij zoeken goede data-mensen, die goede dingen doen. Van die types die je er gewoon heel graag bij wil hebben – net zo goed met mensen als met code!

  • Veel persoonlijke aandacht voor (waarde) groei, inzetbaarheid en “in je kracht komen“ als data professional. Met ook data-inhoudelijke mentoring.
  • Opleiding en kruisbestuiving met andere professionals in state of the art AI skills
  • De kans om in diverse keukens te kijken en veel te leren
  • Leaseauto of NS-Businesscard (ook privégebruik)
  • Gezellige borrels, kennissessies en team-events
  • Collega’s die naast slimme analyses ook goed zijn in slechte grappen


Wij zoeken goede data-mensen, die goede dingen doen. Van die types die je er gewoon heel graag bij wil hebben – net zo goed met mensen als met code!

Title: Data scientist
Description:
Note: Deadline for applying is 23.59 the day before the Job Posting End Date.

UNILEVER IS HIRING Data Scientist /Data Foundation / CD XOPS

Location: Air Office

Full-time

Local conditions apply

The role will be closed once the best match is found even before the posting end date

About Unilever

Unilever is one of the world’s leading suppliers of Food, Home, and Personal Care products, operating in over 190 countries and reaching more than 2 billion consumers every day. Our portfolio includes iconic brands such as Dove, Knorr, Domestos, Hellmann’s, Persil, Cif, Tresemmé, Rexona, and Axe.

Guided by our purpose—to make sustainable living commonplace—we aim to grow our business while addressing the challenges of climate change and human development, enabling people everywhere to live well within the limits of the planet.

About Global Data & Technology (GDT)

Data Foundation within GDT exists to make Unilever data-intelligent, empowering critical business decisions through data, advanced analytics, and AI.

Our vision is a future-fit Unilever—an organisation where data underpins every aspect of decision-making: accelerating innovation, strengthening brands, driving excellence in customer execution, enhancing consumer experiences through personalisation, and transforming internal operations for efficiency and scale.

We bring together specialists across the business and offer a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of data science, business impact, and cutting-edge technology, with a direct link to Unilever’s growth and purpose.

About The Role

As a Data Scientist, you will play a key role in delivering advanced analytical and AI solutions that support commercial and operational decision-making across markets.

This is a hands-on, mid-senior individual contributor role, requiring strong technical depth, the ability to operate autonomously, and close collaboration with product, engineering, and business partners.

Key Responsibilities


Partner closely with product, business, and analytics stakeholders to deeply understand problem statements and translate them into actionable, data-driven solutions.
Design, build, and deploy predictive and prescriptive models using classical statistical methods and machine learning.
Lead fast-paced, agile experimentation, iterating quickly to demonstrate value and inform product development and releases.
Apply design thinking principles to develop solutions that are scalable, usable, and aligned with business needs.
Act as a technical expert in advanced modelling techniques, supporting solution design and peer reviews.
Develop and evolve capabilities in Generative AI and Agentic AI, contributing to next-generation analytics solutions.
Industrialise and scale solutions on cloud-based platforms, ensuring robustness, performance, and maintainability.


Areas of Focus


Build end-to-end data science solutions addressing complex business problems through diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics.
Unlock new insights by integrating internal and external data sources (e.g. retailer data, econometric models, panel data).
Innovate new methodologies driven by local market needs, contributing to the global D&A product pipeline.
Operate primarily as a strong individual contributor, working across multiple markets and teams in close partnership with business interpreters.
Champion continuous improvement through:
Simplified and standardised ways of working
Improved development and delivery practices


About You

Experience & Expertise


Degree qualified in a relevant technical discipline (Data Science, Computer Science, Engineering, Mathematics, Statistics, Econometrics, Operations Research, or similar).
Strong track record of solving complex business problems using quantitative and statistical approaches.
3 yrs or more experience in Deep expertise in several of the following with solid theoretical grounding:
Recommendation engines (Required)
Churn modelling (Required)
Regression and econometric modelling (Preferred)
Time Series Forecasting (Preferred)
Experience developing and industrialising machine learning and advanced analytics solutions.
Advanced proficiency in Python and Spark, with experience working in Databricks and Azure environments.
Proven ability to work with large, complex, high-dimensional datasets, both structured and unstructured (e.g. SAP, POS data).
Exposure to LLMs and agent-based systems is a plus.


Ways of Working


Excellent communication, active listening, and presentation skills, able to engage audiences at different levels of the organisation.
Highly inquisitive and analytical mindset, with a passion for problem solving, simplification, and continuous improvement.
Well-organised, with strong time management skills and the ability to prioritise effectively in a fast-paced, global environment.
Proactive self-starter who thrives with autonomy while collaborating effectively within cross-functional and virtual teams.
Passion for delivering excellence; and developing a culture of “easy to do business with”, where stakeholder’s needs are anticipated
High ethical standards in data handling and decision-making.


WHAT IS IN IT FOR YOU?

Unilever is the place where you can be yourself and bring your purpose to life with the work that you do – creating a better business and a better world. We offer an exciting & dynamic work environment where you can make things happen. Furthermore, we offer some great reward and benefits!


Attractive total remuneration package: excellent company pension, bonus, share scheme.
Flexible cross-disciplinary career opportunities and a wealth of training opportunities & wellbeing resources whenever and wherever.
Plenty of company-paid holidays to further ensure your work-life balance is maintained.
We encourage an inclusive culture, which comes to life with interchangeable public holidays, paid paternity leave of 6 weeks and our transgender policy.
Under the Hybrid Working principles, you will be expected to spend a minimum of 40%-60% in the office or at customers, suppliers or partners to connect and collaborate. For the time you work from home, we will ensure you are well equipped. When you are at the office, you can enjoy our tasty canteen with prepped food and own products.
Informal culture and being the first one trying our new products.
My Fitness Plan (reduction on your Fitness Subscription).
Homework allowance
Company laptop and mobile phone
Green Mobility Policy.


EXCITED?

Join Unilever and our team! To apply, you must do so online. Please do not forget to upload your CV and a motivation letter. Your application will be reviewed against our requirements, and we will be in touch shortly after the closing date to provide you with an update on the status of your application.

Looking forward to meeting you!

Please note this is a Direct Search led by Unilever. Applications from agencies will not be accepted, nor will fees be paid for unsolicited CVs.

Equal Opportunity Employer: Unilever is an Equal Opportunity Employer. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regards to race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, national origin, disability, age, parenthood, pregnancy or any other basis protected by applicable law, and will not be discriminated against.

By highlighting the gender diversity at the workplace, Unilever encourages women equally men to apply. If you look for a job after a long career break or after any type of leave, do not hesitate to apply.

Please save a copy of this vacancy for personal use as it will disappear from the website after closing of the role.

Title: Machine learning engineer
Description:
Machine Learning Engineer

Wil jij als Machine Learning Engineer aan de frontlinie staan van de energietransitie?

Wil jij werken bij een organisatie die een leidende rol in de markt van duurzame energieopslag aanneemt? Kom werken bij Kiwatt en haal het beste uit jezelf.




Wat ga je doen als Machine Learning Engineer?

Je ontwikkelt en onderhoudt verschillende modellen voor optimaal gebruik van onze batterij. Denk hieraan het voorspellen van verschillende energiemarkten, optimaal voorspellen van het verbruik en opwek van de klant en het voorspellen van de performance van de batterij.
Je zoekt steeds naar de juiste oplossing met behulp van Machine Learning, AI, data of heuristiek om zo de meeste waarde toe te voegen aan onze producten.
Je onderhoudt deze modellen en breidt ze uit op basis van marktontwikkelingen en feedback van collega’s en klanten.
Je zorgt ervoor dat de data uit deze modellen ons algoritme voeden zodat de batterij optimaal ingezet wordt.
Samen met onze software engineers bedenk je nieuwe manieren om de sturing van de batterij op de veranderende marktomstandigheden en klantprofielen aan te passen.
Samen met onze software engineers werk je aan verbetering van ons systeem, bijvoorbeeld door predictive maintenance en foutanalyse op basis van interne data.




Werken bij Kiwatt

Kiwatt is opgericht in 2019 en wil energieopslag eenvoudig maken voor ondernemers en particulieren. Kiwatt is een belangrijke speler in de energietransitie door slimme oplossingen aan te bieden voor de opslag van energie. Wij zorgen er voor dat ieder bedrijf zelf controle heeft over de opgewekte energie.




We zijn continu bezig met de doorontwikkeling en optimalisatie van onze producten. Dromen over een duurzame installatie is mooi, maar echt iets doen is beter. Wij zijn geen traditioneel bedrijf, maar innovatief en snel groeiend. We zijn altijd op zoek naar specialisten. De energietransitie is in volle gang en jij kan hier een bijdrage aan leveren!




Nieuwsgierig naar Kiwatt? Kom gewoon eens kijken!




Wie ben jij als Machine Learning Engineer?

• Je hebt een afgeronde HBO- of WO-opleiding op het gebied van Data Science/AI, Wiskunde of Informatica.

• Je hebt een achtergrond in en ervaring met machine learning en data science

Je hebt kennis van de energiemarkt of bent bereid deze snel op te doen

• Je hebt ervaring met programmeren in

• n en ervaring met Machine Learning tools zoals Tensorflow

• Je hebt ervaring met Javascript of bent bereid dit snel te leren.

• Je hebt kennis van MLOps voor versie beheer, testen en in productie brengen van Machine Learning modellen

• Gedrevenheid en passie? Dat heb jij. Natuurlijk ben je blij wanneer je weekend hebt, maar je bent net zo blij als je maandagochtend weer naar het werk mag.

• Je hecht, net als wij, waarde aan kwaliteit. Je wilt iets moois helemaal (af)maken en zorgt voor een perfecte afwerking. De lat ligt hoog en dat is voor jou de gaafste uitdaging.




Werken in ons team

De energietransitie is in volle gang en jij kunt hier als Machine Learning Engineer bij Kiwatt een belangrijke bijdrage aan leveren. Onze producten bestaan uit geavanceerde hard- en software. En hier ligt jouw uitdaging!




Door de implementatie van slimme software draag je bij aan het verbeteren van onze energie-oplossingen. Je weet de wensen van de consument door te vertalen naar praktische oplossingen. Zo versnel je als Machine Learning Engineer met jouw team de energietransitie.




We organiseren regelmatig leuke uitjes, zoals een BBQ. Zo leer je je collega's ook op een andere manier kennen!




Wat krijg je bij ons als Machine Learning Engineer?

• Een salaris tot 4500 euro bruto per maand op basis van een 40-urige werkweek, afhankelijk van de opleiding en ervaring.

• 25 vakantiedagen

• 8% vakantiegeld

• Uitdagende werkzaamheden die jouw technische hart sneller doen kloppen.

• Aandacht voor jouw ontwikkeling en groeipotentieel.

• Een enthousiast en gemotiveerd team van gedreven professionals op een inspirerende locatie.

• Gezellige borrels, BBQ’s en feestjes: we vinden het belangrijk om met elkaar te verbinden.

• € 0,23 per km reiskostenvergoeding.

• Een goede pensioenregeling.




Machine Learning Engineer bij Kiwatt: jouw baan?

Wil je aan de slag bij Kiwatt als Machine Learning Engineer en het allerbeste uit jezelf halen? Laten we dan snel eens kennismaken. Solliciteer eenvoudig via Linkedin.




Bij de vorige openstelling van deze functie was de interesse enorm en ontvingen we een overweldigend aantal reacties. Wil je deze kans niet missen? Reageer dan snel, de vacature sluit op 31 mei 2026.




Nog een vraag? Stel deze aan... Julia van Deelen/ Daan Kampert, E-mailadres: hr@kiwatt.nl. Telefoonnummer: 06-15829757

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Overview

Company Description

VPR Energy BV is part of the Vitol Group, a global leader in the energy and commodities sector. With key operations in Houston, London, Singapore, Rotterdam, and 40 other offices worldwide, Vitol serves the world’s energy markets. Vitol invests globally in traditional and sustainable energy assets, including 18 million mm³ of storage, 850+ kbpd of refining capacity, and more than 7,000 service stations.

VPR Energy

Located in the Europoort-Rotterdam area, VPR Energy BV operates the most dynamic refinery owned by the world’s largest independent energy and commodities trading company. Our highly efficient and flexible 140 kbpd topping refinery processes gas condensate and crude oil into naphtha, jet fuel, gasoil, and residual fuel. These valuable products serve as feedstock in the petrochemical industry or as energy to heat homes and fuel marine vessels.

Every day, we leverage our expertise in refining crude and condensate blends, striving to be best in class in terms of energy reductions and sustainability targets. Our operations are managed from our office in Europoort Rotterdam by a small, highly engaged team with extensive knowledge and experience in processing, operations, and logistics. Our people build our reputation as a leading player in the energy markets through their creativity, commitment, professionalism, and entrepreneurial spirit.

As VPR Energy continues to grow steadily, we are seeking the next member to join our innovative team.

Job Description

Position Summary

The Data Scientist will act as an important support for the wider VPR performance team, using expertise to improve and enhance business activities. Core responsibilities include:


Writing and implementing algorithms to extract, structure, and analyze data
Applying existing software and tools, or developing new ones (including new data science methods and techniques) to transform unstructured, raw data into usable business information
Translating insights obtained from data into concrete recommendations for business operations in written reports, dashboards, or presentations
Managing and enhancing data streams and analyses
Automating tasks such as data collection


Qualifications

Qualifications and Experience


Minimum Bachelor’s degree in a STEM field (for example econometrics, engineering, or mathematics)
2 – 5 years of relevant working experience
Strong database programming skills (such as Python and SQL) and excellent knowledge of Excel
Experience with CI/CD processes (e.g., Git)
Ability to convey complex analysis in a concise and understandable manner
Enjoys working in a dynamic environment
A good command of the Dutch and English language
No 9-to-5 mentality


Additional Information

Additional Information


Contribute directly to a large variety of projects
Be part of a small and flat organization with lots of creative freedom
Free in-house lunch, freshly prepared and catered daily
Potential for travel to our Geneva and London office
Competitive compensation and benefits package

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
My client within the Finance sector in The Hague are on the look out for a Data Scientist to join their team.




The role is ideal for a junior Data Scientist looking to improve their skills by being guided by the best in class.




The ideal candidate is enthusiastic and had a growth mindset to improve.




Requirements:

• Strong academic background. Ideally a masters in a relevant field (AI, Computer Science, Maths, Physics etc.)

• Knowledge of forecasting is ideal but not required

• 0-3 years of industry experience

• Python and SQL knowledge.




Salary Package:

• Up to €70,000 per annum (D.O.E)

Title: Junior Data Scientist
Description:
At brainbay, we’re redefining real estate with data-driven solutions. As an NVM subsidiary, we manage the Netherlands’ largest real estate database, leveraging AI and machine learning to enhance transparency, efficiency, and accessibility in the real estate market. As our Junior Data Scientist you will develop analytical tools and solutions for real estate agents and valuators.

Full-time Utrecht / hybride

Job Description

Your role as a Junior Data Scientist


As our Junior Data Scientist you will develop analytical tools and solutions for real estate agents and valuators. You are involved in building predictive statistical and machine learning models and in implementing generative AI solutions. As part of an experienced team, you will work in sprint cycles to deliver the desired models and applications. Also, you will be active in maintaining and improving our current suite of models and in implementing cutting-edge ML Ops solutions.
You are also able to translate the needs of our real estate agents and other customers into useful research and analysis. You will collaborate with researchers and other data scientists to conduct the analyses for publications and you will support business developers to pitch new products and explain the models to possible clients.
Examples of recent projects include ‘making a bidding tool to quantify the probability that a bid on a house is the winning bid’, ‘leveraging LLM’s in order to enrich data on commercial real estate listings’ and ‘predicting the step in energy-label after upgrading a set of sustainability characteristics of a house’.


Job Requirements

What we are looking for:


We are looking for a team player who flourishes in an ambitious and informal environment. You have a good knowledge of different machine learning model types and have hands-on data science experience with Python. You have strong statistical knowledge and like the combination of data science, ML Ops and research or you want to further develop/explore this.
You are always looking for continuous improvement and you have a great willingness to share and absorb knowledge. Your mindset is proactive, problem-solving and you are passionate about quality and delivering added value for the customer. Keeping up-to-date knowledge of the real estate sector and data science topics comes natural to you and you love to bring your knowledge into practice.


Key Qualifications


Master’s degree in computer science, AI, econometrics, applied mathematics, statistics or similar quantitative field
Minimum 1-2 years of experience in a data science role
Proficiency in Python and SQL
Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines, ML Ops tools, containerization and APIs
Experience working with cloud platforms
Strong knowledge of statistical and machine learning models
Eager to learn as part of an experienced team
Ability to explain complex concepts verbally as well as in writing in (mostly) Dutch and in English
A valid work permit for working in The Netherlands is required (and you already reside in The Netherlands).


Benefits Include


Start salary range € 4.000 – 5.000,- monthly, depending on skills and experience
8,25% holiday allowance
13th-month bonus
37 vacation days per year
Zwitserleven pension plan with 4% employee and 8% employer contribution
Travel allowance (€0.23 per km) or 100% reimbursed via NS Business Card
Hybrid working with a €2.45 daily allowance for home working days
€1.500 annual training budget for growth opportunities
Tech essentials: laptop and mobile phone (or €30 net monthly bring your own device)


About Brainbay

At brainbay, we’re redefining real estate with data-driven solutions. As an NVM subsidiary, we manage the Netherlands’ largest real estate database, leveraging AI and machine learning to enhance transparency, efficiency, and accessibility in the real estate market. Our mission is to empower professionals with innovative tools that simplify complex processes and unlock valuable insights.

Join a diverse team of over 50 forward-thinking professionals dedicated to pushing boundaries in technology and data. Our modern office in Utrecht offers free parking, hybrid work opportunities, and a vibrant environment focused on collaboration and work-life balance.

When interested please apply here directly via this NVM recruitee weblink.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Data Scientist

Location: Netherlands (Hybrid)

Salary: €90K - €120k + bonus

Contract: Fixed-Term

HL1106




Finatal are partnering with a leading international technology business to appoint a Senior Data Scientist. Sitting within a central data function, this role will focus on delivering end-to-end Data Science and AI initiatives across multiple business units and markets. You will take ownership of projects from opportunity identification and requirements gathering through to machine learning model development, AI and LLM implementation, production deployment and communicating business impact. Alongside the technical delivery, you will work closely with senior stakeholders to translate complex analysis into measurable commercial outcomes across growth, customer engagement, pricing and operational performance.




Role:

• Own the end-to-end delivery of Data Science and AI projects, from problem definition through to deployment and adoption.

• Build and implement machine learning models and analytical solutions that drive commercial outcomes across areas such as conversion, retention, pricing, and customer behaviour.

• Partner closely with Product, Growth, Commercial and Operational stakeholders to identify opportunities and prioritise initiatives.

• Translate complex technical findings into clear, actionable recommendations for business leaders and decision-makers.

• Design and implement AI, Machine Learning and LLM-driven solutions that improve decision-making and deliver measurable commercial outcomes.

• Contribute to the development of scalable Data Science and AI capabilities within a fast-paced, international environment.




Requirements:

• Strong hands-on Data Science experience with advanced Python and SQL skills, experience building and deploying Machine Learning models in production environments, and exposure to cloud platforms and large-scale data processing technologies such as PySpark.

• Experience working with AI, Generative AI and LLM-based applications, applying them to solve real business and operational challenges.

• Proven ability to own projects end-to-end, from problem definition and stakeholder engagement through to deployment and measurable business impact.

• Strong stakeholder management and communication skills, with experience translating complex technical concepts into actionable recommendations.




If you are interested in learning more about this opportunity, please share your CV to Harriet at Harriet.lander@finatal.com.

Title: Data Scientist
Description:
Unlock your potential at Accenture

Are you ready to turn raw data into actionable insights and shape the future of decision-making? At Accenture, we’re searching for a Data Scientist Consultant to join our dynamic team. If you have 3-5 years of experience and a passion for problem-solving, innovation and collaboration, we’re excited to meet you.

What We Do At Accenture

Accenture is a global leader in consulting and technology services. We help businesses harness the power of data to drive innovation and deliver value. By combining industry expertise with cutting-edge technology, we empower our clients to make data-driven decisions and achieve their goals. With over 700,000 colleagues worldwide, our network thrives on creativity, inclusivity, and collaboration—and we’re just getting started…

What you’ll do:



Discover insights: Analyze complex datasets to uncover trends, patterns, and opportunities that drive business transformation.

Build smarter models: Develop machine learning models and algorithms to solve challenging business problems.

Collaborate to innovate: Work with diverse teams to design and implement scalable data solutions.

Experiment and evolve: Stay ahead of the curve by exploring the latest tools and techniques in AI and data science.

Drive real impact: Partner with our clients to create solutions that are practical, innovative, and aligned with their strategic goals.



What we’re looking for:



Experience: 3-5 years in data science, machine learning, or related fields. Familiarity with tools like Python, R, and SQL is essential.

Skills: Proficiency in statistical modeling, predictive analytics, and visualization tools such as Tableau or Power BI. Also exceptional written and spoken skills in Dutch & English. Experience with cloud platforms (AWS, Azure, GCP) is a plus.

Mindset: A curious problem-solver with a passion for turning data into stories.



What we offer:



Impactful work: Collaborate with Fortune 500 companies and solve real-world problems.

Career growth: Access world-class training, certificates, mentorship, career coaching and opportunities to grow your expertise.

Flexibility: Enjoy a hybrid work model that supports your work-life balance.

Recognition: Be part of a team that values your contributions and celebrates your successes.

Inclusive culture: Thrive in an environment where every voice is valued, and innovation is a team sport.



These promises are reflected through our excellent employee benefits, including:



Flexible working hours (4x9) & work location, FlexMoment leave of absence program (50% paid leave of absence for 3 months) and FlexDay; a flexible day you can take off to celebrate what matters to you – from Eid al-Fitr to a birthday.

Paid leave: Parental leave, Partner leave for all non-birthing parents, Bereavement leave, and Gender affirmation leave.

Flexible mobility benefits: electric bicycle, car lease, or a mobility budget for public transport;

Pension scheme, Accenture shares, annual bonus, collective health insurance scheme

Social activities like Friday Drinks, sport events and parties



Why Accenture?

At Accenture, we blend technology and human ingenuity to create smarter solutions for our clients and the world. We’re not just a workplace—we’re a community where ideas flourish, careers grow, and meaningful impact happens every day.

Let’s build the future together.

Ready to turn data into powerful insights and drive real-world impact? Join Accenture’s expert data science team to work on cutting-edge AI, ML, and analytics solutions. Apply now and shape the future with data!

Title: Associate Scientist
Description:
Over het bedrijf

Onze opdrachtgever is een internationale organisatie binnen de diergezondheidssector die zich richt op de ontwikkeling, productie en distributie van innovatieve vaccins en gezondheidsoplossingen voor dieren. Vanuit moderne onderzoeks- en productiefaciliteiten werken multidisciplinaire teams dagelijks aan producten die bijdragen aan het welzijn van dieren wereldwijd.

Binnen deze sterk gereguleerde omgeving staan kwaliteit, veiligheid, innovatie en continue verbetering centraal. Onderzoek en ontwikkeling spelen een belangrijke rol bij het realiseren van nieuwe producten en het optimaliseren van bestaande processen.




Over de afdeling

Je komt terecht binnen een onderzoeks- en ontwikkelafdeling die zich richt op de ontwikkeling, optimalisatie en ondersteuning van biologische productieprocessen voor vaccins. Hier wordt gewerkt aan zowel nieuwe productontwikkelingen als het verbeteren van bestaande processen en producten.

De afdeling bestaat uit wetenschappers, specialisten en projectteams die nauw samenwerken met Productie, Quality Control, Technical Services en andere R&D-afdelingen. Binnen deze omgeving krijg je de mogelijkheid om je verder te ontwikkelen op het gebied van biologische procesontwikkeling, vaccinontwikkeling en procesoptimalisatie.




Over de functie

Als Associate Scientist ben je verantwoordelijk voor het uitvoeren van laboratoriumwerkzaamheden gericht op de ontwikkeling, optimalisatie en ondersteuning van biologische productieprocessen. De focus ligt hierbij op antigen- en FPU-processen die een belangrijke rol spelen binnen de ontwikkeling en productie van vaccins.

Je werkt aan het verbeteren van bestaande processen, ondersteunt bij de ontwikkeling van nieuwe productiemethoden en draagt bij aan de succesvolle opschaling van processen richting productie. Hierbij combineer je praktische laboratoriumwerkzaamheden met data-analyse, procesoptimalisatie en samenwerking met multidisciplinaire projectteams.

Binnen deze functie krijg je de ruimte om initiatief te nemen, verbeteringen voor te stellen en jezelf verder te ontwikkelen als specialist binnen biologische procesontwikkeling en vaccinproductie.




Wat ga je doen?

Als Associate Scientist houd je je bezig met een breed scala aan onderzoeks- en ontwikkelingsactiviteiten, waaronder:

Ontwikkelen, optimaliseren en ondersteunen van antigen- en FPU-productieprocessen.
Uitvoeren van experimenten gericht op productontwikkeling, procesverbetering en technische ondersteuning van bestaande productieprocessen.
Opzetten van experimentele ontwerpen en testplannen in samenwerking met projectleiders.
Zelfstandig uitvoeren, plannen en coördineren van laboratoriumexperimenten.
Uitvoeren van procesontwikkelingsstudies gericht op opbrengst, robuustheid en reproduceerbaarheid van biologische processen.
Ondersteunen bij de opschaling van processen van laboratoriumschaal naar pilot- en productieschaal.
Analyseren, interpreteren en rapporteren van onderzoeksresultaten en procesdata.
Signaleren van afwijkingen, onderzoeken van oorzaken en voorstellen doen voor verbeteringen van processen en proefopzetten.
Vastleggen van resultaten in laboratoriumdocumentatie en bespreken van bevindingen binnen projectteams.
Opstellen van protocollen, rapportages, technische documenten en onderzoeksverslagen.
Ondersteunen bij pilotbatches, consistentiebatches, seedproducties en andere GxP-gerelateerde activiteiten.
Evalueren en implementeren van nieuwe technieken en methodieken voor procesontwikkeling.
Actief bijdragen aan veiligheid, kwaliteit en continue verbetering binnen het laboratorium.




Wat breng je mee?

Een afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding in Life Sciences, Biotechnologie, Biologie, Microbiologie of een vergelijkbare richting.
Ervaring binnen een laboratoriumomgeving, bij voorkeur binnen onderzoek, ontwikkeling of productieondersteuning.
Kennis van GxP-richtlijnen en ervaring met werken binnen een gereguleerde omgeving.
Affiniteit met of ervaring in biologische procesontwikkeling, vaccinontwikkeling of biotechnologische productieprocessen.
Kennis van antigen-, fermentatie-, celkweek- of FPU-gerelateerde processen is een sterke pré.
Ervaring met procesoptimalisatie, experimenteel ontwerp en data-analyse.
Een gestructureerde en nauwkeurige manier van werken met oog voor kwaliteit en data-integriteit.
Sterke analytische vaardigheden en een probleemoplossende instelling.
Goede organisatorische vaardigheden en het vermogen om meerdere werkzaamheden gelijktijdig te beheren.
Een proactieve houding en een sterke focus op continue verbetering.
Goede communicatieve vaardigheden in zowel Nederlands als Engels.
Een sterke focus op veiligheid, samenwerking en resultaatgericht werken.




Wat bieden wij?

Een uitdagende functie binnen een innovatieve internationale organisatie.
De mogelijkheid om direct bij te dragen aan de ontwikkeling en verbetering van vaccins die wereldwijd worden ingezet voor diergezondheid.
Veel ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, opleiding en doorgroeimogelijkheden.
Samenwerking binnen multidisciplinaire en internationale projectteams.
Een professionele werkomgeving waarin kwaliteit, veiligheid en innovatie centraal staan.
Toegang tot moderne laboratoria en geavanceerde technologieën.
Een aantrekkelijk salaris passend bij jouw kennis en ervaring.
Goede secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden.
Een collegiale werksfeer waarin kennisdeling, samenwerking en continue verbetering worden gestimuleerd.




Interesse?

Ben jij een scientist met interesse in biologische procesontwikkeling, vaccinproductie en innovatieve technologieën? Wil je werken in een omgeving waar wetenschap en praktijk samenkomen en waar jouw werk direct bijdraagt aan de gezondheid van dieren wereldwijd? Neem dan contact op met Nadir Lamtalssi via telefoonnummer 088 890 0843 of mail naar nadir.lamtalssi@balans.nu. Hij staat je graag te woord.




Werk maken van dromen?

Wij hebben vele vacatures openstaan voor uiteenlopende functies, zoals laborant, analist, productontwikkelaar en kwaliteitsmedewerker. Onze opdrachtgevers zijn actief binnen de levensmiddelenindustrie, farmacie, chemie en andere productiebedrijven. Ben jij een laborant of analist op (k)mlo-, hlo-, wo-, of PhD-niveau, óf heb je een levensmiddelenopleiding (mas, has of wo) afgerond, schrijf je dan in bij Balans en maak werk van je dromen.

Binnen deze omgeving krijg je de mogelijkheid om je verder te ontwikkelen op het gebied van biologische procesontwikkeling, vaccinontwikkeling en procesoptimalisatie.


Over de functie

Als Associate Scientist ben je verantwoordelijk voor het uitvoeren van laboratoriumwerkzaamheden gericht op de ontwikkeling, optimalisatie en ondersteuning van biologische productieprocessen. De focus ligt hierbij op antigen- en FPU-processen die een belangrijke rol spelen binnen de ontwikkeling en productie van vaccins.

Je werkt aan het verbeteren van bestaande processen, ondersteunt bij de ontwikkeling van nieuwe productiemethoden en draagt bij aan de succesvolle opschaling van processen richting productie. Hierbij combineer je praktische laboratoriumwerkzaamheden met data-analyse, procesoptimalisatie en samenwerking met multidisciplinaire projectteams.

Binnen deze functie krijg je de ruimte om initiatief te nemen, verbeteringen voor te stellen en jezelf verder te ontwikkelen als specialist binnen biologische procesontwikkeling en vaccinproductie.


Wat ga je doen?

Als Associate Scientist houd je je bezig met een breed scala aan onderzoeks- en ontwikkelingsactiviteiten, waaronder:

  • Ontwikkelen, optimaliseren en ondersteunen van antigen- en FPU-productieprocessen.
  • Uitvoeren van experimenten gericht op productontwikkeling, procesverbetering en technische ondersteuning van bestaande productieprocessen.
  • Opzetten van experimentele ontwerpen en testplannen in samenwerking met projectleiders.
  • Zelfstandig uitvoeren, plannen en coördineren van laboratoriumexperimenten.
  • Uitvoeren van procesontwikkelingsstudies gericht op opbrengst, robuustheid en reproduceerbaarheid van biologische processen.
  • Ondersteunen bij de opschaling van processen van laboratoriumschaal naar pilot- en productieschaal.
  • Analyseren, interpreteren en rapporteren van onderzoeksresultaten en procesdata.
  • Signaleren van afwijkingen, onderzoeken van oorzaken en voorstellen doen voor verbeteringen van processen en proefopzetten.
  • Vastleggen van resultaten in laboratoriumdocumentatie en bespreken van bevindingen binnen projectteams.
  • Opstellen van protocollen, rapportages, technische documenten en onderzoeksverslagen.
  • Ondersteunen bij pilotbatches, consistentiebatches, seedproducties en andere GxP-gerelateerde activiteiten.
  • Evalueren en implementeren van nieuwe technieken en methodieken voor procesontwikkeling.
  • Actief bijdragen aan veiligheid, kwaliteit en continue verbetering binnen het laboratorium.


Wat breng je mee?

  • Een afgeronde hbo- of wo-opleiding in Life Sciences, Biotechnologie, Biologie, Microbiologie of een vergelijkbare richting.
  • Ervaring binnen een laboratoriumomgeving, bij voorkeur binnen onderzoek, ontwikkeling of productieondersteuning.
  • Kennis van GxP-richtlijnen en ervaring met werken binnen een gereguleerde omgeving.
  • Affiniteit met of ervaring in biologische procesontwikkeling, vaccinontwikkeling of biotechnologische productieprocessen.
  • Kennis van antigen-, fermentatie-, celkweek- of FPU-gerelateerde processen is een sterke pré.
  • Ervaring met procesoptimalisatie, experimenteel ontwerp en data-analyse.
  • Een gestructureerde en nauwkeurige manier van werken met oog voor kwaliteit en data-integriteit.
  • Sterke analytische vaardigheden en een probleemoplossende instelling.
  • Goede organisatorische vaardigheden en het vermogen om meerdere werkzaamheden gelijktijdig te beheren.
  • Een proactieve houding en een sterke focus op continue verbetering.
  • Goede communicatieve vaardigheden in zowel Nederlands als Engels.
  • Een sterke focus op veiligheid, samenwerking en resultaatgericht werken.


Wat bieden wij?

  • Een uitdagende functie binnen een innovatieve internationale organisatie.
  • De mogelijkheid om direct bij te dragen aan de ontwikkeling en verbetering van vaccins die wereldwijd worden ingezet voor diergezondheid.
  • Veel ruimte voor persoonlijke ontwikkeling, opleiding en doorgroeimogelijkheden.
  • Samenwerking binnen multidisciplinaire en internationale projectteams.
  • Een professionele werkomgeving waarin kwaliteit, veiligheid en innovatie centraal staan.
  • Toegang tot moderne laboratoria en geavanceerde technologieën.
  • Een aantrekkelijk salaris passend bij jouw kennis en ervaring.
  • Goede secundaire arbeidsvoorwaarden.
  • Een collegiale werksfeer waarin kennisdeling, samenwerking en continue verbetering worden gestimuleerd.


Interesse?

Ben jij een scientist met interesse in biologische procesontwikkeling, vaccinproductie en innovatieve technologieën? Wil je werken in een omgeving waar wetenschap en praktijk samenkomen en waar jouw werk direct bijdraagt aan de gezondheid van dieren wereldwijd? Neem dan contact op met Nadir Lamtalssi via telefoonnummer 088 890 0843 of mail naar nadir.lamtalssi@balans.nu. Hij staat je graag te woord.


Werk maken van dromen?

Wij hebben vele vacatures openstaan voor uiteenlopende functies, zoals laborant, analist, productontwikkelaar en kwaliteitsmedewerker. Onze opdrachtgevers zijn actief binnen de levensmiddelenindustrie, farmacie, chemie en andere productiebedrijven. Ben jij een laborant of analist op (k)mlo-, hlo-, wo-, of PhD-niveau, óf heb je een levensmiddelenopleiding (mas, has of wo) afgerond, schrijf je dan in bij Balans en maak werk van je dromen.

Title: Machine Learning Scientist
Description:
About the Role:

For a multinational client in Amsterdam, we are looking for a Machine Learning Scientist on an interim basis. You will be responsible for the development and production of Machine Learning models.




Responsibilities:

Design, build, and iterate on Generative AI and NLP solutions using LLMs and modern ML techniques
Drive experimentation, define clear success metrics, and rigorously evaluate model performance
Develop and optimise prompt engineering strategies and model workflows for production use
Work on text-based AI use cases
Start from base or foundation models, simplifying and adapting them for specific product use cases
Translate complex AI concepts into clear, understandable solutions for cross-functional stakeholders




Requirements:

Experience with end-to-end Machine Learning workflows (building models and putting them into production)
Strong NLP and Generative AI experience, particularly with LLM-based systems
Hands-on experience with model fine-tuning, evaluation, and experimentation
Solid understanding of prompt engineering and production-oriented model design
Ability to simplify complex problems and focus on pragmatic, scalable solutions
Comfortable working in an environment that prioritises impact over academic research
Based in Amsterdam (no sponsorship offered)




What We Offer:

An interim position
Flexible Working: Hybrid model, with 2–3 days in the office

Title: Senior Data Scientist
Description:
Samba is a media intelligence company. We know what the world is watching, reading, and thinking about — in real time, at scale, across every screen. Our data exists with the consent of over a billion people, organized into the most complete picture of consumer attention ever built. The biggest brands in the world use that picture to make smarter decisions. We think it’s the most interesting data asset on the planet, because it’s the most culturally relevant. 







We are hiring a Senior Data Scientist in Amsterdam to help shape Samba TV's data science function for the agentic era of advertising. You will define modeling methodology, build production ML systems, and apply modern AI and agentic capabilities across our data products.




You bring 8+ years of experience, deep expertise in machine learning and modern AI, and the technical range to take methodology from research through production deployment. You are an active collaborator with engineering, product, and partner teams, and an advocate for rigorous, defensible modeling practice.







What You'll Do
Modeling and ML Development






Own end-to-end delivery of data science projects, from problem scoping through production deployment.








Define and ship modeling methodology that powers Samba's data products, including model selection, evaluation frameworks, and reproducibility standards.








Apply solid command of core ML and statistics (regression, classification, clustering, model evaluation, experimental design, causal inference) to billion-row, real-world data.








Build production-quality Python and PySpark on Databricks: well-tested, documented, reusable.








Partner with Data Engineering to define data requirements, validate pipelines, and ensure model inputs are reliable and production-ready.






AI and Agentic Capabilities






Build and operate advanced AI systems using modern methodologies: retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), LLM-augmented modeling and Graph Neural Networks. Design AI-driven modeling approaches that improve as signals evolve, supporting agentic decision-making at platform scale.








Integrate LLMs and agentic workflows into production ML pipelines where they extend modeling capability and unlock new product surfaces.






Technical Contribution and Collaboration






Drive technical design for modeling components within your scope, producing clear solution documents covering problem statement, approach, metrics, and trade-offs.








Translate business requirements into modeling solutions in close collaboration with product, engineering, and go-to-market partners.








Uphold high standards for production-quality data science.








Mentor data scientists on the team through structured feedback, pairing, and design review.






MLOps and Production Practice






Establish and operate MLOps practices: experiment tracking, pipeline orchestration (Airflow), model monitoring, retraining workflows, and reproducibility standards.








Apply privacy-compliant data handling practices, including GDPR, CCPA, and Samba's data governance policies.











Who You Are
Required






8+ years of hands-on data science experience with a Bachelor's degree in Statistics, Data Science, Computer Science, Mathematics, or a related quantitative field (or 6+ years with a Master's, 3+ years with a PhD, or equivalent).








Demonstrated ability to own and deliver complex, multi-sprint data science projects from problem scoping through production deployment.








Solid command of core ML and statistics, including neural networks, regression, classification, clustering, model evaluation, experimental design, and causal inference, applied to billion-row datasets.








Track record of building methodology, not just applying it: data analysis, model selection, evaluation frameworks, and solid documentation of decision processes








Production experience with vector databases (Pinecone, Weaviate, Milvus, pgvector, or equivalent) for retrieval, matching, or inference at scale.








Advanced Python with production-quality, tested code; strong SQL and PySpark on billion-row datasets.








Databricks, Delta Lake, and job orchestration (Airflow); hands-on production experience on AWS, GCP, and Databricks.








MLOps proficiency: experiment tracking, pipeline orchestration, model monitoring, reproducible deployment.








Experience designing and operating agentic AI systems in production: prompt engineering, agent orchestration, tool use, or integration of LLMs into ML pipelines.








A clear communicator who translates technical work into design docs, user stories, and cross-functional conversations.








An active mentor who invests in others, gives direct feedback, and raises the bar for the team as a whole.






Preferred






Knowledge graph design (RDF, OWL, SPARQL, or equivalent graph frameworks), Natural Language Processing, Background in ad tech, CTV/OTT, ACR, audience activation, identity resolution, or measurement methodologies.








Experience with causal inference (A/B testing, synthetic control, uplift modeling).














Base salary is just one component of Samba total compensation package for employees. Other rewards may include bonuses, short-term incentives, and long-term incentives. In addition, Samba provides health insurance, wellness offerings, life and disability insurance, a retirement savings plan, paid holidays and paid time off (PTO), and other employee benefits.




Samba is an equal opportunity employer. We celebrate diversity and are committed to creating an inclusive environment for all employees.  We strive to empower connection with one another, reflect the communities we serve, and tackle meaningful projects that make a real impact.




 




Samba may collect personal information directly from you, as a job applicant, Samba may also receive personal information from third parties, for example, in connection with a background, employment or reference check, in accordance with the applicable law. For further details, please see Samba's Applicant Privacy Policy. For residents of the EU , Samba Inc. is the data controller.




We may use artificial intelligence (AI) tools to support parts of the hiring process, such as reviewing applications, analyzing resumes, or assessing responses. These tools assist our recruitment team but do not replace human judgment. Final hiring decisions are ultimately made by humans. If you would like more information about how your data is processed, please contact us.

Title: Data Scientist (Supply Chain), Singapore
Description:
Date: 17 Jun 2026

Country: Singapore

Location:

MarinaOne, SG

Function: Digital & Information Technology

About Us

As a leading provider of high-quality food and beverage ingredients, we work with farming communities across the globe to grow, source and produce ingredients that are good for consumers, farmers, and the world around us. We supply household food brands and manufacturers worldwide with cocoa, coffee, dairy, nuts and spices ingredients which are often grown on our own farms and estates and sourced from hundreds of thousands of farmers across ~50 countries. Along with our diverse manufacturing and innovation capabilities, this means we can provide ingredients for a range of products, from a plant-based latte mix to an almond based snack bar or a dairy-free ice cream.  Making a positive impact on people and planet is a core component of our Purpose, to be the change for good food and a healthy future. With a deep-rooted presence in the countries where our ingredients are grown, we are closer to farmers, enabling better quality, and more reliable, traceable, and transparent supply. And whoever we’re with, whatever we’re doing, we always make it real.

The data scientist is responsible to provide descriptive, diagnostic, predictive, and prescriptive analytics for ofi’s business operations including manufacturing, procurement, sales, consumer insights, sustainability, trading, supply chain, etc.

Key Responsibilities


Work closely with business users in analysing large and/or complex data sets, identifying patterns, and developing algorithms to extract insights.
Designing and implementing machine learning models to solve complex business problems. This includes selecting appropriate algorithms, feature engineering, model training, and evaluation.
Develop and apply optimization techniques such as linear programming, mixed-integer programming, and simulation-based optimization to improve decision-making in supply chain, manufacturing, and logistics.
Create compelling visualizations that communicate the results of data analysis to both technical and non-technical stakeholders. Use visualization tools such as Power BI or Tableau to create interactive dashboards and reports.
Managing data science projects from conception to completion. This includes scoping projects, setting timelines, managing resources, and communicating project status to stakeholders.
Maintaining and monitoring analytical models after production deployment.
Thought leadership including staying up to date with the latest developments in data science. Continuous engagement with business users to update them on potential opportunities of data science initiatives for their business.


Qualifications And Skills


MS/PhD in computer science, statistics, or operations research or related technical discipline (e.g. math).
Minimum 5 to 8 years of work experience in a statistical and/or data science role, with deep knowledge of machine learning, statistics, and optimization.
Preferred programming experience: Python & SQL with considerable experience with a wide range of tools including SparkML, H2O, FlinkML, TensorFlow, Pandas, R and Matlab.
Proficient in working with large and complex data sets using modern data platforms such as Snowflake and distributed data processing tools like PySpark.
Hands-on experience with simulation and optimization modeling using tools such as Gurobi.
Data visualization experience in Power BI and/or Tableau.
Excellent written and verbal communication skills along with strong desire to work in cross-functional teams.
Strategic mindset and the ability to identify opportunities to use data to drive business growth. You should be able to think creatively and identify innovative solutions to complex problems.
Ability to lead end-to-end data science projects and work with business leadership to quickly turn insights into actions.


ofi is an equal opportunity employer and values diversity. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to racial or ethnic origin, color, age, religion or belief, sex, nationality, disability, sexual orientation, gender identity, gender expression, genetic information, or any other characteristic protected by applicable law.

Applicants are requested to complete all required steps in the application process including providing a resume/CV in order to be considered for open roles.

Title: AI Data Scientist
Description:
Only applicants who speak dutch will be reviewed




Deze specialistische rol richt zich op het ontwikkelen en toepassen van AI en data science oplossingen die directe waarde leveren voor de organisatie, klanten en eindgebruikers. Je fungeert als verbindende schakel tussen business, IT en centrale stakeholders en vertaalt complexe data en modellen naar concrete, toepasbare inzichten. De rol combineert analytische diepgang met advies, implementatie en samenwerking binnen een internationale context.




Over de rol

In deze rol identificeer je AI-toepassingen en beoordeelt je de waarde en haalbaarheid daarvan, denk aan impact op winst, klanttevredenheid en processen. Je ontwikkelt voorspellende modellen met machine learning en statistische technieken en analyseert grote hoeveelheden gestructureerde en ongestructureerde data om strategische en operationele vraagstukken op te lossen.




Je begeleidt het volledige traject van idee tot implementatie en doorloopt daarbij de vastgestelde governance stappen voor AI en data science use cases. Daarnaast signaleer je RPA-mogelijkheden, coördineer je aanvragen en onderzoek je hoe AI-technologieën deze processen naar een hoger niveau kunnen tillen.




Je ondersteunt de organisatie bij AI-gerelateerde tools zoals Copilot en Power Automate, verzorgt trainingen, adviseert over effectieve prompts en berekent de concrete voordelen daarvan. Experimenteren met nieuwe technologieën om de data science capabilities binnen de organisatie te blijven ontwikkelen is een vast onderdeel van de functi




Over jou

HBO of WO werk en denkniveau
Minimaal 2 jaar ervaring binnen AI en data science, ervaring met change of transformatie is een pre
Ervaring met tools en technologieën zoals Python, R, SQL, Git, Databricks, Dataiku, Domino, VS Code en data visualisatie tooling
Kennis van grote databronnen en datamodellen zoals Hadoop, Spark en SQL
Ervaring met het ontwikkelen van modellen, simulaties, scenario analyses en algoritmes
Sterk analytisch vermogen en in staat om complexe materie helder uit te leggen aan niet-technische stakeholders
Proactief, gestructureerd en resultaatgericht
Vloeiend in Nederlands en Engels in woord en geschrift
Woonachtig in Nederland




Werkomgeving

Je maakt onderdeel uit van een team dat zich richt op business transformatie en procesverbetering. Je werkt samen met collega's binnen data, technologie en efficiency disciplines in een professionele, collegiale en multidisciplinaire setting.




Over Levy Professionals

Sinds 2000 leveren wij professionele oplossingen aan organisaties variërend van tech start ups tot gevestigde ondernemingen. Vanuit onze kantoren in Amsterdam en Londen hebben wij een internationaal en lokaal netwerk opgebouwd van hooggekwalificeerde professionals en contractors, gedreven door de focus op het verbinden van expertise aan complexe opdrachten. Onze aanpak is gebaseerd op langdurige relaties en professionele integriteit.

Je begeleidt het volledige traject van idee tot implementatie en doorloopt daarbij de vastgestelde governance stappen voor AI en data science use cases. Daarnaast signaleer je RPA-mogelijkheden, coördineer je aanvragen en onderzoek je hoe AI-technologieën deze processen naar een hoger niveau kunnen tillen.


Je ondersteunt de organisatie bij AI-gerelateerde tools zoals Copilot en Power Automate, verzorgt trainingen, adviseert over effectieve prompts en berekent de concrete voordelen daarvan. Experimenteren met nieuwe technologieën om de data science capabilities binnen de organisatie te blijven ontwikkelen is een vast onderdeel van de functi


Over jou

  • HBO of WO werk en denkniveau
  • Minimaal 2 jaar ervaring binnen AI en data science, ervaring met change of transformatie is een pre
  • Ervaring met tools en technologieën zoals Python, R, SQL, Git, Databricks, Dataiku, Domino, VS Code en data visualisatie tooling
  • Kennis van grote databronnen en datamodellen zoals Hadoop, Spark en SQL
  • Ervaring met het ontwikkelen van modellen, simulaties, scenario analyses en algoritmes
  • Sterk analytisch vermogen en in staat om complexe materie helder uit te leggen aan niet-technische stakeholders
  • Proactief, gestructureerd en resultaatgericht
  • Vloeiend in Nederlands en Engels in woord en geschrift
  • Woonachtig in Nederland


Werkomgeving

Je maakt onderdeel uit van een team dat zich richt op business transformatie en procesverbetering. Je werkt samen met collega's binnen data, technologie en efficiency disciplines in een professionele, collegiale en multidisciplinaire setting.


Over Levy Professionals

Sinds 2000 leveren wij professionele oplossingen aan organisaties variërend van tech start ups tot gevestigde ondernemingen. Vanuit onze kantoren in Amsterdam en Londen hebben wij een internationaal en lokaal netwerk opgebouwd van hooggekwalificeerde professionals en contractors, gedreven door de focus op het verbinden van expertise aan complexe opdrachten. Onze aanpak is gebaseerd op langdurige relaties en professionele integriteit.

Title: Junior Data Scientist – Forecasting & Optimization – Startersfunctie (0-1 jaar ervaring)
Description:
Over TOC

TOC is een groeiend energy trading & optimization bedrijf in Rotterdam. Wij optimaliseren dagelijks grote energie-assets voor onze klanten door software, data en operationele expertise te combineren. Onze ambitie: betrouwbare, transparante en volledig geautomatiseerde optimalisatie op het hoogste niveau.




De rol

Dit is een startersfunctie specifiek bedoeld voor recent afgestudeerden (MSc, max 1 jaar werkervaring). Je krijgt een gedegen inwerktraject en wordt begeleid door ervaren collega's.




Als Junior Data Scientist Forecasting werk je mee aan forecasting-modellen die direct input leveren voor trading- en optimalisatiestrategieën. Je ontwikkelt en verbetert voorspellingen voor energieproductie en prijsmarkten en groeit stap voor stap richting meer verantwoordelijkheid voor onderdelen van de forecasting-keten die directe impact hebben op trading performance.




Je werkt nauw samen met senior data scientists en het Quant-team en krijgt ruimte om te leren en mee te denken. Je werkt doelgericht en krijgt steeds meer eigenaarschap naarmate je groeit in de rol.




Verantwoordelijkheden

Ontwikkelen en verbeteren van forecasting-modellen
(energieproductie, EPEX, onbalans, intraday, aFRR)
Data-ingestie, feature engineering en model training/validatie
Integratie met de backtesting-omgeving en analyse van P&L-impact
Monitoren van modelperformance in productie en voorstellen doen voor verbeteringen
Werken in een omgeving waarin modellen 24/7 operationeel zijn




Profiel

MSc (of bijna afgerond) in Data Science, Econometrie, Wiskunde, Natuurkunde of vergelijkbaar (recent afgestudeerd, max 1 jaar werkervaring)
Sterke kennis van statistiek en tijdreeksanalyse
Sterke technische vaardigheden in Python (pandas, NumPy; ML is een plus – ervaring vanuit studie of persoonlijke projecten)
Interesse in energie- en/of financiële markten
Leergierig, proactief en enthousiast om te groeien in een professionele omgeving
Comfortabel met werken in een 24/7 operatieomgeving




Arbeidsvoorwaarden

💰 Salarisrange: €40.000 – €55.000 afhankelijk van ervaring

🏖️ 31 vakantiedagen

💼 Pensioenregeling, onkostenvergoeding, OV-kaart

🏢 Hybride werken (min. 2 dagen per week op kantoor)

Taal: Nederlands
